author: @aga5897
text:
Given the Global spread of your fans, it might be a better idea to have a Starter Course, which would include a 'starter-pack' of ink, graphene, current-collectors etc that would be required to replicate the experiments demonstrated in a utoob video, using the same 'starter pack'.

That should make it cheaper, and certainly more Accessible to all of us folks spread all over the planet (= in different timezones).

I'd certainly pay for something like that - an FWG Chemistry/Battery Set with Accompanying Video(s) would be awesome !

replies.author: @pattimurray-smith6905
replies.text: Thanks for that - there will be just those kinds of packs available as rewards in the Indegogo campaign....the workshops are a response to requests - though we do realise that most of those requests came from nearer to home!

replies.author: @aga5897
replies.text:
You're already sitting on a vast Global audience who WANT to play with your stuff, and will happily pay something.

If i can pre-buy a Starter Pack even before the videos/packs exist, i'll send you 100 euros  right now if you send me a paypal address.

Indegogo, meh. They are great for things that do not work at all.
I think your stuff Will work, so indegogo is a bad avenue.

replies.author: @pattimurray-smith6905
replies.text: Thank you - the indegogo campaign should be running by the end of next week. We are all working as quickly as we can to get everything done in every direction!

replies.author: @michaelkelly7932
replies.text: I'm all for a kit or something, but even that might be a strain financially... I even make my wife and kids watch your videos...

author: @K9JT
text: I would be very much interested but I live in South Africa and would not be able to afford it yet. I hope that it kicks off successfully though so that it's still running by the time I can afford it. Having said that though, a subscription based online academy (suggested by another comment) is an even better and more sustainable idea.

replies.author: @pattimurray-smith6905
replies.text: Thanks for your interest and ideas!

author: @EmunahCreative
text: Online webinars (or live streaming from your local workshops with archived videos) are much better for those of us who can't commute across the globe. Why not just create an FWG  Online Academy?

replies.author: @larrystrayer8336
replies.text: Yes. An online webinar would be of interest to me also.

replies.author: @pattimurray-smith6905
replies.text: As we've mentioned below, we may look at webinars later on, but the workshops are a response to requests - and at least a start.  Thank you for your interest.

author: @TheBaconWizard
text: Hi guys. Money is going to be a little tight for me this year (I'm a student, now!) so my attendance would be dependent on some outside factors. But I think the price is reasonable, and funds permitting I would definitely want to do it. It also partly depends where you are actually based as I'd be using public transport.

replies.author: @pattimurray-smith6905
replies.text: We're in Canterbury, Kent. Thanks for your interest.

replies.author: @TheBaconWizard
replies.text: Patti Murray-Smith OK, that's doable by coach, I'm definitely interested :)

author: @senna4281
text: Hi yes this sounds like a good idea and I would love to attend. But some questions. How many people do you think could attend your workshop at once. Are you looking at just a small groups 5-10 or more. Also is there any scope to have over more days. As has been said if your are traveling some distance then more than two days would be great.

replies.author: @pattimurray-smith6905
replies.text: We need 6 to make it viable and would have trouble catering for more than 8 in the lab.  We need to keep it to 2 days because we have so many projects on that would otherwise fall too far behind - but there's nothing to stop people hanging around after the two days....

replies.author: @pattimurray-smith6905

replies.author: @pattimurray-smith6905
replies.text: A one-week workshop wouldn't be possible for us because there are so many other things going on that we have to keep going at the same time....but there's nothing to stop you hanging around before or after the workshop! Thank you for your interest.
